headline: GOLD JUMPS TO $4,749 AS TRUMP EXTENDS IRAN CEASEFIRE; Silver Surges 2.5% to $77.50
snippet: Spot gold rose 0.8% to $4,748.58 on Wednesday after President Trump indefinitely extended the U.S.-Iran ceasefire, easing fears of an inflation spike from prolonged high oil prices. Silver spiked 2.5% to $77.50 per ounce alongside the de-escalation signal, though analysts note the ceasefire was declared unilaterally and Iran has yet to confirm.

headline: FORTUNE BAY DRILLS 9.9 g/t Au OVER 7.0m IN 135-METRE STEP-OUT AT SASKATCHEWAN BOX DEPOSIT
snippet: Fortune Bay Corp (TSXV: FOR) drilled hole B26-350 at its Goldfields Gold Project in Saskatchewan, returning 3.7 g/t Au over 21.0 metres including 9.9 g/t Au over 7.0 metres — a 135-metre step-out that places mineralization 140 metres outside and below the current open-pit constrained resource, with the zone wide open at depth.

headline: F3 URANIUM HITS MULTI-HOLE STEP-OUTS AT TETRA ZONE; Structural Link to High-Grade JR Zone Confirmed 13km North
snippet: F3 Uranium Corp intersected uranium mineralization in three of six winter 2026 step-out holes at the Tetra Zone on its Patterson Lake North project in the Athabasca Basin, Saskatchewan. New structural analysis and uranium age-dating confirm the Tetra Zone shares the same multi-phase hydrothermal system as the high-grade JR Zone 13km to the north, materially expanding the district's discovery potential.

headline: USA RARE EARTH PAYS $2.8 BILLION FOR BRAZIL'S SERRA VERDE — Only Outside-Asia Supplier of All 4 Magnetic Rare Earths
snippet: USA Rare Earth (NASDAQ: USAR) announced a $300M cash plus $2.5B stock deal to acquire Serra Verde Group, owner of the Pela Ema mine in Brazil — currently the only large-scale producer outside Asia capable of supplying neodymium, praseodymium, dysprosium, and terbium at scale. The combined company holds a 15-year U.S. government offtake agreement for 100% of its magnetic rare earth output, with Serra Verde projected to deliver $550–$650M in annualized EBITDA by end-2027.

headline: DRYDEN GOLD MAPS 12KM ANOMALY CORRIDOR AT HYNDMAN, Stakes 12,000 Additional Hectares to Secure Strike Extensions
snippet: Dryden Gold Corp (TSXV: DRY) established a district-scale gold footprint at the Hyndman Property with a 12km by 2.5km continuous gold-in-till anomaly corridor aligned with the Wabigoon deformation zone. Gold mineralization was intersected in all six initial drill holes, and the company immediately staked an additional 12,000 hectares along strike to secure untested targets ahead of an aggressive 2026 drill program.

headline: SILVER HEADING FOR SIXTH STRAIGHT DEFICIT IN 2026 — 46M-Oz Shortfall as 762M Oz Drawn from Global Stocks Since 2021
snippet: The Silver Institute and Metals Focus project a 46.3-million-troy-ounce silver market deficit for 2026 — the sixth consecutive annual shortfall — with physical investment demand rising 20% year-over-year. Since 2021, 762 million ounces have been drawn from above-ground inventories to cover the cumulative gap, steadily tightening the available supply buffer for the next demand spike.

headline: CHINA IMPORTED 790+ TONNES OF SILVER IN EARLY 2026 — Gold SWOT Flags Structural Demand Floor Driven by Solar
snippet: China's early-2026 silver imports exceeded 790 tonnes, driven by a surge in solar panel fabrication and industrial offtake. GoldSeek's weekly Gold SWOT analysis argues that Chinese demand is creating a structural floor for silver prices independent of investment sentiment — with the country's solar build-out absorbing supply that would otherwise soften the market.

headline: MINERS FINALLY BEATING THE METAL: GDX's Re-Rating Could Be 2026's Most Underappreciated Trade
snippet: After years of lagging gold's price gains, gold mining stocks are entering a fundamental re-rating cycle in 2026 as record margins, compressed multiples, and persistent institutional underweighting converge. Analysts argue that miners generating outsized free cash flow at $4,700+ gold are still priced as if gold is at $2,500 — and that the multiple expansion trade is only beginning.

headline: GOLDMAN HOLDS COPPER AT $12,650/MT — But Traxys Sees $15,000 as DRC Disruption and China Acid Ban Loom
snippet: Goldman Sachs reaffirmed its 2026 copper average price forecast of $12,650 per metric tonne on April 21 alongside a 490,000-tonne surplus estimate, while commodities trader Traxys is targeting $15,000/t citing supply risks. The divergence hinges on two wildcards: DRC inventory rundowns that could curtail 125,000 tonnes if logistics disruptions persist past May, and China's sulphuric acid export ban threatening 200,000 tonnes of Chilean production.

headline: LITHIUM CARBONATE UP 125% FROM JUNE 2025 TROUGH — But Energy Storage Boom May Finally Be the Real Demand Driver
snippet: Battery-grade lithium carbonate has surged more than 125% from its June 2025 trough entering Q2 2026, though analysts remain divided on whether the rally reflects genuine structural recovery or speculative front-running. A new demand pillar has emerged — stationary energy storage — which unlike EV demand does not track consumer confidence cycles, potentially providing a more durable floor under lithium prices.

headline: RECORD GOLD PRICES SENDING BIG MONEY HUNTING FOR THE NEXT JUNIOR DISCOVERY — Exploration Budgets at 14-Year High
snippet: With gold trading near $4,750 after a record-setting Q1, institutional and retail capital is rotating into junior gold explorers on the TSX-V and ASX in search of leveraged exposure to the metal. Analysts note that exploration budgets are rising sharply for the first time since 2012, with royalty and streaming companies increasing deal flow toward early-stage projects at grades that would have been uneconomic three years ago.
